headlines.headline: Major 7.5-Magnitude Earthquake Strikes Off Japan, Tsunami Warning Issued
headlines.summary: A powerful 7.5-magnitude earthquake struck off the northeastern coast of Japan, prompting a tsunami warning with waves of up to 3 meters expected. Bullet train operations were halted and residents were urged to evacuate coastal areas.

headlines.headline: Italy Discusses Expanding EU Naval Mission to Protect Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Italy's defense minister said discussions are underway to expand the EU's Aspides naval mission to include the Strait of Hormuz, warning that Iran's missile and nuclear ambitions pose a global threat. The minister stressed that Asian countries should assume greater responsibility given the strait's vital importance to their economies.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria's Pro-Russian Former President Wins Election by Landslide
headlines.summary: Pro-Russian former President Rumen Radev has won Bulgaria's election by a landslide, official results showed, potentially pushing the EU and NATO member state closer to Moscow. Radev may secure a parliamentary majority, ending years of weak coalition governments.

headlines.headline: China Warns Joint US-Philippines-Japan Drills Risk Regional Trust
headlines.summary: China warned that joint military drills involving the US, Philippines and Japan risk undermining regional trust, as more than 17,000 troops launched the Balikatan exercises. Beijing said the Asia-Pacific region needs peace and tranquility, not the introduction of external forces.

headlines.headline: Iran Studied Ukraine War to Sharpen Drone and AI Capabilities Before US Operation
headlines.summary: Iranian military commanders closely studied the war in Ukraine before launching operations against the US, focusing on drones, 3D printing and AI-powered systems. The FT reports Tehran applied lessons from Kyiv's asymmetric warfare to prepare for conflict with Washington.

headlines.headline: Iran to Send Negotiation Team to Islamabad Tuesday Despite Blockade Tensions
headlines.summary: An Iranian delegation will arrive in Islamabad on Tuesday for a second round of peace talks, Pakistani official sources told Nikkei Asia, despite Tehran's earlier statements that it would not send negotiators while the US navy maintains its blockade. The mediator says the focus is on ceasefire extension as 'complex issues' require more time.

headlines.headline: Asian Financial Regulators Sound Alert Over Anthropic's Mythos AI Security Risks
headlines.summary: Regulators across Asia are stepping up scrutiny of cybersecurity risks in financial systems as concerns spread over Anthropic's latest AI model Mythos. Singapore's financial regulator is urging banks to plug holes, while South Korea convened an emergency meeting after Anthropic found the model capable of discovering security holes that had gone undetected for years.

headlines.headline: Germany Begins Privatization of Seized Gazprom Unit SEFE
headlines.summary: The German government will begin the official privatization of SEFE, the state entity formed from Gazprom's seized German unit. Berlin plans to raise 1.5-2 billion euros to expand the company's energy infrastructure, with SEFE having already secured long-term LNG supply deals with Norway's Equinor and UAE's ADNOC to reduce dependence on Russian gas.

headlines.headline: Asia's EV Revolution Accelerates as Iran War Oil Shock Hits Pump Prices
headlines.summary: With Brent crude hovering around $100 per barrel amid the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz disruption, Asian nations heavily dependent on oil imports are accelerating their shift to electric vehicles. Nepal now ranks second globally for EV sales share at over 70% of new vehicle sales, as the combination of abundant hydropower and high fossil fuel taxes makes petrol cars a luxury few see the point in buying.

headlines.headline: Iran Agrees to Attend Second Round of US Talks Despite Hormuz Hostilities
headlines.summary: An Iranian delegation is expected to arrive in Islamabad on Tuesday for the second round of talks with the US, according to Pakistani sources, despite ongoing Strait of Hormuz hostilities. The talks come as the fragile ceasefire faces strain from the US seizure of an Iranian cargo vessel and mutual accusations of violations.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian Drone Attack on Russia's Tuapse Port Kills One
headlines.summary: A major Ukrainian drone attack on Russia's Black Sea port of Tuapse sparked a fire and killed at least one person, Russian officials said, just hours after a blaze was doused in the wake of a similar attack on April 16. The strike is part of Kyiv's campaign targeting Russian energy export infrastructure.

headlines.headline: National Australia Bank Flags $503 Million Impairment Hit From Mideast Volatility
headlines.summary: Australia's largest business lender said it expects credit impairment charges to double to A$706 million ($503 million) in the first half, as the Iran war roils the global economy and financial markets. The announcement reflects growing concerns about the conflict's impact on banking sector stability.

headlines.headline: Bank Indonesia to Hold Rates at 4.75% Through 2026 as Iran War Fuels Inflation Risks
headlines.summary: Bank Indonesia is set to keep its key policy rate unchanged at 4.75%, according to a Reuters poll of 31 economists, with many paring bets for cuts this year as an Iran war-fuelled energy shock pushes up inflation and pressures the rupiah. Emerging markets face tightening monetary conditions amid the crisis.

headlines.headline: European Shares Set to Open Lower as Hopes for US-Iran Peace Fade
headlines.summary: Futures tracking Europe's main stock indexes fell more than 1% as hopes for peace in the Middle East ebbed with tensions reigniting after Washington seized an Iranian cargo ship that tried to run its blockade and Tehran vowed to retaliate. European markets face uncertainty as the ceasefire appears increasingly fragile.

headlines.headline: UAE Bourses Ease on Renewed US-Iran Tensions
headlines.summary: UAE markets retreated in early trade as fears deepened that the ceasefire between Washington and Tehran could unravel after the US seized an Iranian cargo vessel, while traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remained largely suspended. Gulf equities remain volatile amid the ongoing crisis.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Condemns 'Tyrants' Spending Billions on Wars During Africa Visit
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV criticized 'tyrants' who spend billions on wars during his visit to conflict-hit Cameroon, saying 'the world is being ravaged by a handful of tyrants.' The pontiff also criticized foreigners who exploit Africa's wealth for profit, days after a public spat with President Trump over the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Iran Executes Two Men Convicted of Links to Israel's Mossad Spy Agency
headlines.summary: Iran executed two men convicted of cooperating with Israel's Mossad intelligence service and planning attacks inside the country. The two, identified as Mohammad Masoum Shahi and Hamed Validi, were accused of belonging to a spy network and had received training abroad including in Iraq's Kurdistan region.

headlines.headline: Four Candidates Vie to Lead Embattled UN Amid Global Crises and Budget Woes
headlines.summary: Four candidates are competing to succeed Antonio Guterres as UN Secretary-General, facing unprecedented global instability, wars and a crushing budget crisis. Chile's Michelle Bachelet, Argentina's Rafael Grossi, Costa Rica's Rebeca Grynspan, and Senegal's Macky Sall will face grillings by 193 member states.

headlines.headline: US and Philippines Kick Off Major Combat Drills Despite Iran War Focus
headlines.summary: The United States and the Philippines kicked off one of their largest combat exercises Monday, demonstrating continued US military commitment to the Indo-Pacific region despite Washington's focus on the Iran war. The annual Balikatan exercises come amid rising tensions with China in the South China Sea.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Criticizes Foreigners Exploiting Africa's Wealth During Cameroon Visit
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV criticized foreigners who 'in the name of profit, continue to lay their hands on the African continent to exploit and plunder it' during his visit to Bamenda, Cameroon. The pontiff addressed 20,000 worshippers in a city at the center of a brutal separatist rebellion that has killed at least 6,000 people.

headlines.headline: North Korea Tests Ballistic Missiles With Cluster Munition Warheads
headlines.summary: North Korea test-launched five Hwasong-11 Ra ballistic missiles armed with cluster bomb and fragmentation mine warheads, with Kim Jong Un and his daughter observing. The tests highlight Pyongyang's push to develop weapons capable of penetrating US and South Korean defenses, potentially inspired by cluster munitions use in the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Iran Rejects Second Round of US Peace Talks Citing 'Excessive Demands'
headlines.summary: Iran's state news agency IRNA denied participation in new peace talks with the US, citing Washington's 'excessive demands, unrealistic expectations' and the continued naval blockade. The diplomatic setback came as JD Vance and Jared Kushner were reportedly preparing to travel to Islamabad for negotiations.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Surge 6% as US-Iran Standoff Blocks Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Oil prices jumped more than 6% in early trading after Iran reversed its decision to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. US crude rose to $87.90 per barrel and Brent climbed to $95.64, wiping out much of Friday's 9% decline as renewed doubts emerged about when the vital waterway will fully reopen.

headlines.headline: Trump Says US Seized Iranian Cargo Ship After Blockade Breach Attempt
headlines.summary: President Trump said US forces seized the Iranian-flagged cargo vessel Touska after it ignored warnings and attempted to breach a US naval blockade. Iran's military called the seizure 'armed piracy' and vowed retaliation, as the fragile ceasefire set to expire Wednesday faces growing strain from mutual accusations of violations.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Deploys Robots to Replace Human Soldiers in Combat Assault Missions
headlines.summary: Ukraine's military is increasingly using robots to replace human soldiers in combat, including assault missions. The unmanned ground vehicles and drones allow Kyiv to preserve manpower while maintaining pressure on Russian forces, marking a significant evolution in modern warfare tactics.

headlines.headline: Ukraine PM Says IMF Agreed Raising Tax on Self-Employed Is 'Not Constructive'
headlines.summary: Ukrainian Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko said the IMF agreed during Washington talks that introducing VAT tax on self-employed individuals was 'not constructive' given Ukraine's wartime economic pressures. The issue has been contentious as Kyiv seeks to balance IMF reform demands with domestic political sensitivity.

headlines.headline: Gold Falls as Dollar Strengthens on Renewed US-Iran Tensions
headlines.summary: Gold prices fell as the dollar firmed amid renewed US-Iran tensions, with the Strait of Hormuz closure pushing oil prices higher and reviving inflation fears. The precious metal's decline reflects investor flight to the dollar as Middle East uncertainty persists.

headlines.headline: Global Leftists Rally in Barcelona to Counter Far-Right Resurgence
headlines.summary: Thousands of leftists gathered in Barcelona over the weekend to brainstorm ways to halt the rise of authoritarianism, discussing reforms from UN Security Council restructuring to billionaire taxation. The summit aims to build momentum against the far-right's electoral gains across Europe and the Americas.

headlines.headline: Analysis Questions Whether Strait of Hormuz Will Open Anytime Soon
headlines.summary: A consistent theme in global oil markets since the US-Israel attack on Iran has been that the Hormuz closure will be short-lived. However, with Iran reversing course on reopening and hardliners gaining influence in Tehran, analysts are questioning whether optimism about a rapid resolution is warranted.

headlines.headline: Pro-Russian Former President Radev Claims Victory in Bulgaria's Parliamentary Elections
headlines.summary: Russia-friendly former president Rumen Radev is on course to win Bulgaria's parliamentary elections with a potential outright majority, a result that could stabilize the country but shift the EU and NATO member's foreign policy orientation toward Moscow amid the ongoing geopolitical realignment.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria's Pro-Russian Radev Set for Landslide Win in Eighth Election in Five Years
headlines.summary: Exit polls put Rumen Radev's Progressive Bulgaria coalition at 37%, more than double the score of the closest runner-up. The former president, who has opposed EU sanctions on Russia, may need to form a coalition but could fundamentally alter Bulgaria's stance within NATO and the European Union.

headlines.headline: Zelensky Condemns US Sanctions Waiver Giving Russia Billions for War Effort
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky condemned the easing of sanctions on Russian oil after the United States extended a waiver allowing Moscow to sell crude already loaded on vessels. Zelensky warned that Russia's shadow fleet of 110 tankers would bring billions to fund the war against Ukraine.

headlines.headline: The Guardian Live Coverage: US Officials Head to Pakistan as Hormuz Crisis Deepens
headlines.summary: The Guardian's live coverage tracks the deepening Middle East crisis as US officials prepare to travel to Pakistan for negotiations amid the Iran-US standoff. The Strait of Hormuz remains a critical flashpoint with global energy supplies at stake.

headlines.headline: World Weighs Fate of Mideast Ceasefire After US Seizes Iranian Cargo Ship
headlines.summary: The US military said it fired on an Iranian-flagged cargo ship as the vessel sailed toward Iran's Bandar Abbas port, while Iran's military said the ship had been traveling from China. The seizure throws the fragile ceasefire into question as negotiators head to Pakistan for talks.

headlines.headline: Oil Price Plunge on US-Iran Peace Hopes Gives Indian Markets Some Breather
headlines.summary: The Indian rupee and government bonds will be comforted by a drop in oil prices even as shipping through the Strait of Hormuz remains at a standstill, days before a ceasefire between the United States and Iran is set to expire.

headlines.headline: Wall Street Set to Wade Deeper Into Earnings Season as Stocks Hit Record Highs
headlines.summary: Investors will look to a heavy week of US corporate results to further fuel a stunning rebound in the stock market, which has shaken off war-related concerns to reach record peaks despite ongoing Middle East tensions.

headlines.headline: US Officials Attend IMF Meetings in 'Parallel Reality' as World Grapples With Oil Crisis
headlines.summary: The IMF's spring meetings buzzed with talk of a 13 million barrel per day oil shortfall reshaping economies, rates and defense alliances, yet US officials treated it as a sideshow. The disconnect highlights how counting on US leadership to resolve crises is no longer the guarantee it had long been.

headlines.headline: Trump Issues Iran Ultimatum as Bulgaria Votes and Robots Race Ahead
headlines.summary: US President Donald Trump accused Iran of a 'total violation' of the ceasefire, threatened to wipe out Iran's bridges and power plants, and said his envoys will arrive in Pakistan on Monday for more talks. Meanwhile, Bulgaria's pro-Russian former president appears set for election victory.

headlines.headline: Man Kills 8 Children in Louisiana Mass Shooting, Deadliest US Attack in Two Years
headlines.summary: A gunman killed eight children aged 1 to 14 in domestic-related shootings at two homes in Louisiana before being shot dead by police. It was the deadliest mass shooting in the United States in more than two years, igniting debates over gun violence.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria's Pro-Russia Former President Radev Wins Landslide Election Victory
headlines.summary: Progress Bulgaria, the Kremlin-friendly coalition led by former President Rumen Radev, is on track to win 37-38% of the vote according to exit polls. The result could end years of weak coalition governments and shift the EU member's foreign policy orientation toward Moscow.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Carries Out First-Ever Shahed Interception Using Naval Drone Platform
headlines.summary: Ukraine's military carried out the first-ever interception of a Russian Shahed drone using a naval drone platform operated by the 412th Nemesis Brigade. The breakthrough demonstrates Kyiv's growing innovation in maritime defense capabilities against Russian aerial threats.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Strikes Russian Oil Refinery in Krasnodar Krai, Major Fire Reported
headlines.summary: Ukrainian drones struck the Tuapse Oil Refinery in southern Russia's Krasnodar Krai during an overnight attack, causing a major fire. Kyiv continues its campaign to disrupt Moscow's war funding by targeting Russian energy infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Memorial Day 2026: 174 IDF Soldiers and 79 Israeli Civilians Killed in Past Year
headlines.summary: Israel's Memorial Day 2026 commemorates 174 IDF soldiers and 79 Israeli civilians killed over the past year, a significant increase reflecting the ongoing multi-front war. The toll underscores the human cost of the Iran war and Gaza conflict.

headlines.headline: UK Police Investigate Iran Links to Arson Attacks on Jewish Sites in London
headlines.summary: British police are investigating possible Iranian links to a series of arson attacks on Jewish targets in London, with the UK chief rabbi warning of a 'sustained campaign of violence' against the Jewish community gathering momentum.

headlines.headline: US Military Strike Kills Three on Alleged Drug Boat in Caribbean
headlines.summary: The US military killed three people in a strike on a boat accused of ferrying drugs in the Caribbean Sea, continuing the Trump administration's campaign against 'narcoterrorism' that has now killed at least 181 people. Critics have questioned the legality of the boat strikes amid continued operations despite the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Restoring Oil Flows Through Hormuz Will Take Months or Years Even After War Ends
headlines.summary: Even if the guns fall silent, oil and gas flows through the Strait of Hormuz will take months—possibly years—to recover to pre-war levels. The stop-start shipping traffic underscores profound uncertainty hanging over the world's most critical energy chokepoint.

headlines.headline: US Navy Destroyer Disables Iranian Cargo Ship Breaking Blockade
headlines.summary: The USS Spruance fired on the Iranian-flagged M/V Touska after a six-hour standoff when the vessel attempted to reach Bandar Abbas in violation of the US naval blockade. Marines boarded the ship, which remains in US custody, while Iran vowed retaliation calling it 'armed piracy.'

headlines.headline: Ukraine Unveils New Koral Surface-to-Air Missile for Domestic Air Defense
headlines.summary: Ukraine displayed what appears to be the Koral medium-range surface-to-air missile during a defense industry exhibition, with an estimated range of 100km and anti-ballistic missile capability. The domestically developed SAM could help offset Ukraine's shortage of Western air defense systems.

headlines.headline: Pakistani Journalist Says Iran's Military Strength Delivered Strategic Results
headlines.summary: A senior Pakistani journalist argues Iran achieved strategic gains in its war with the US and Israel, including establishing control over the Strait of Hormuz and potentially accelerating the reduction of the American military footprint in the Middle East. Pakistan mediated the ceasefire and continues hosting negotiations.

headlines.headline: Iran Will Never Cede Control of Strait of Hormuz, Senior Politician Tells BBC
headlines.summary: Iranian lawmaker Ebrahim Azizi told the BBC that Tehran will 'never' give up control of the Strait of Hormuz, calling it an 'inalienable right.' He said Iran is introducing a bill to enshrine its control over the strategic waterway in law, viewing it as a permanent asset rather than just a bargaining chip in negotiations.

headlines.headline: Zelensky Condemns US Extension of Russian Sanctions Waiver Amid Energy Crisis
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky condemned the US decision to extend a waiver allowing Russia to sell oil already loaded on vessels until May 16. Zelensky said 'every dollar paid for Russian oil is money for the war,' warning that Russia's shadow fleet of 110 tankers would bring $10 billion to Moscow's coffers.

headlines.headline: US Businesses Can Begin Claiming Tariff Refunds Monday After Supreme Court Ruling
headlines.summary: A refund system for businesses that paid tariffs the Supreme Court ruled unconstitutional is set to launch Monday. Over 330,000 importers paid about $166 billion on 53 million shipments, with 56,497 importers eligible for $127 billion in refunds including interest. The process is expected to take 60-90 days per approved claim.

headlines.headline: Asia's Energy Pain Far From Over as LNG Shock Hits World's Factory
headlines.summary: Asian economies are grappling with tightened LNG supply, mounting foreign exchange pressure and damage already locked into Q2 performance as the Iran war disrupts 20% of global oil and LNG flows. The Philippines' GDP growth was downgraded to 3.7% from projected 5-6%, with the IMF warning of 'permanent scarring' across the region.

headlines.headline: India Faces Fertiliser Crisis as Iran War Disrupts Gulf Supplies
headlines.summary: India's urea imports have surged to $935 per tonne from $508 pre-war, with ammonia facilities in Qatar and Saudi Arabia shut after Iranian strikes. Domestic urea production has fallen from 2.5 million tonnes monthly to just 1.5 million tonnes due to LNG supply disruptions, threatening the kharif crop season.

headlines.headline: Australia's Big Banks Brace for Oil Shock Fallout as Hormuz Remains Blocked
headlines.summary: Australia's major lenders Westpac and National Australia Bank are increasing bad loan provisions as the prolonged Hormuz closure begins impacting the economy. The government has announced a $1 billion emergency loan program administered through the big four banks to help businesses weather the fuel crisis.

headlines.headline: North Korea Tests Cluster Warhead Missiles With Kim Jong Un Observing
headlines.summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un supervised test-launches of the upgraded Hwasongpho-11 Ra tactical ballistic missile with cluster bomb and fragmentation mine warheads. Five missiles struck a 12.5-13 hectare target area with 'very high density,' demonstrating the regime's continued weapons development amid global distractions.

headlines.headline: Spain Urges EU to End Association Agreement With Israel Over International Law Violations
headlines.summary: Spain announced it will ask the European Union to end its association agreement with Israel over alleged violations of international law. The move marks a significant escalation in European diplomatic pressure on Israel amid the broader Middle East conflict.

headlines.headline: Japan's Taiwan Strait Warship Transit on Treaty Anniversary Raises New Flashpoint With China
headlines.summary: A Japanese warship's transit of the Taiwan Strait on the anniversary of a key security treaty drew sharp criticism from Beijing, which said the passage 'harmed the feelings of the Chinese people.' Tensions in East Asia reached a fresh peak amid competing maritime claims.

headlines.headline: Japan Self-Defense Forces to Join Philippine-US Military Drills in South China Sea
headlines.summary: Japan's Self-Defense Forces will take part in annual military drills led by the Philippines and the US, testing Tokyo's commitment to security in the South China Sea. The exercises come as Japan expands its regional defense partnerships amid growing tensions with China.

headlines.headline: Bahrain Reviews Citizenship Laws After Foiling Iran-Linked Cells
headlines.summary: Bahrain's King Hamad announced measures against those who 'betrayed' the country after authorities foiled Iran-linked cells. The Gulf state is reviewing citizenship laws, potentially stripping nationality from dissidents, amid heightened regional tensions with Iran.

headlines.headline: Two US Embassy Officials Die in Car Crash in Mexico After Drug Operation
headlines.summary: Two US embassy officials died in a car crash in Chihuahua alongside two Mexican agents following a drug operation. The officials were trainers working with Mexican counterparts as part of ongoing bilateral security cooperation efforts.

headlines.headline: CBS Reports on Operation to Remove Iran's Highly Enriched Uranium Stockpile
headlines.summary: A 60 Minutes report examines the challenges of securing Iran's estimated 1,000 pounds of 60%-enriched uranium stored deep underground at Isfahan. Experts say the operation would require thousands of US troops and weeks on the ground, with any deal needing Iran's cooperation.

headlines.headline: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Again, Says Traffic Control Until War Ends
headlines.summary: Iran has again closed the Strait of Hormuz, reversing a brief reopening and saying it will maintain 'supervision and control' over traffic until the war definitively ends. Iran's chief negotiator stated: 'It is impossible for others to pass through the Strait of Hormuz while we cannot.'

headlines.headline: Iran Replenishing Missile Launchers Faster Than Before War, Guards Commander Says
headlines.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guards Aerospace Force commander said the country now updates and replenishes its missile and drone launchers at a higher speed than before the war with the US and Israel. The statement signals Iran's continued military capacity despite weeks of strikes.

headlines.headline: France and Greece to Renew Defense Pact for Five Years Amid Iran War
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron will travel to Greece next week to renew a security pact between the two countries for another five years, a Greek government official said. The renewal comes against the backdrop of the war in Iran and shifting European security dynamics.

headlines.headline: Israeli Soldier Filmed Smashing Jesus Statue in Lebanon Sparks Outrage
headlines.summary: An image showing an Israeli soldier using a jackhammer to smash a statue of Jesus in southern Lebanon went viral, sparking outrage including among US conservatives. The incident in the Maronite Christian village of Debel drew condemnation from figures like Marjorie Taylor Greene and Matt Gaetz, potentially damaging Israel's support among evangelical Christians.

headlines.headline: Iran Vows 'Swift Response' After US Seizes Iranian Cargo Ship Near Hormuz
headlines.summary: Iran's military vowed retaliation after US Marines seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship near the Strait of Hormuz. Trump said the Navy 'blew a hole in the engineroom' of the vessel Touska. Meanwhile, Brent crude surged 7% to $96.85 as markets reacted to renewed Hormuz uncertainty.

headlines.headline: Chinese Humanoid Robot Beats Human Record in Half-Marathon by 6 Minutes
headlines.summary: A humanoid robot named Lightning, developed by Chinese company Honor, completed a half-marathon in Beijing in 50 minutes 26 seconds—beating the human world record by over 6 minutes. The race attracted 105 robot competitors, up from 21 last year, showcasing China's rapid advances in humanoid robotics and autonomous navigation.

headlines.headline: Iranian National Charged in US With Trafficking Arms to Sudan for Tehran
headlines.summary: US federal prosecutors charged Iranian national Shamim Mafi with brokering the sale of drones, bombs, and millions of rounds of ammunition manufactured by Iran and sold to Sudan. Mafi, a lawful US resident since 2016, was arrested at Los Angeles International Airport and faces up to 20 years in prison if convicted.

headlines.headline: Ukraine's Long-Range Strikes Inflicted $2.3 Billion in Russian Oil Losses in March
headlines.summary: Ukrainian estimates show long-range strikes on Russian oil infrastructure caused at least $2.3 billion in lost oil revenue for Russia in March alone, Zelensky said. The attacks disabled about 40% of Russia's oil export capacity as Ukraine targets the Kremlin's key revenue source.

headlines.headline: Gulf Equities Mixed as Hormuz Uncertainty Caps Ceasefire Optimism
headlines.summary: Gulf equities ended mixed on Sunday after President Trump said Iran had committed a serious violation of the ceasefire, renewing uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz and the state of US-Iran talks despite his insistence that a peace deal would happen. Markets remain cautious amid conflicting signals.

headlines.headline: Dollar Rebounds as Middle East Tensions Reignite, Hormuz Closed
headlines.summary: The dollar headed for its highest level in a week at the start of Asian trading as renewed tensions in the Middle East sent investors scurrying for safe havens. Iran's reversal on reopening the Strait of Hormuz sparked fresh risk aversion.

headlines.headline: Oil Jumps, Stock Futures Slip as Iran Tensions Unsettle Markets
headlines.summary: Oil prices jumped, the US dollar rose and stock futures fell as investors dealt with conflicting messages about the Iran war and news that the Strait of Hormuz was closed again. The renewed uncertainty overshadowed ceasefire hopes.

headlines.headline: Kim Jong Un Oversees Test of New Cluster Warhead Missile as North Korea Accelerates Weapons Program
headlines.summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un oversaw test launches of upgraded short-range ballistic missiles including the Hwasong-11 Ra equipped with a cluster warhead, state media KCNA reported. The test demonstrates Pyongyang's continued weapons development despite international pressure.

headlines.headline: IMF Cuts 2026 Global Growth Outlook, Warns of Recession Risk From Iran War
headlines.summary: The International Monetary Fund has trimmed its global growth forecast for 2026, warning that a prolonged Iran war could significantly worsen the outlook if energy prices surge further. The revision reflects growing economic uncertainty from the Middle East conflict.

headlines.headline: Michigan Attorney General Rejects Trump Administration Ballot Request in Election Push
headlines.summary: Michigan's attorney general is rejecting an effort by the US Justice Department to obtain ballots and other voting materials, pushing back against what critics call a broader administration effort to challenge election administration.

headlines.headline: Trump Approval Rating Hits Record Low in NBC News Poll as Iran War Drags On
headlines.summary: A new NBC News poll shows the war in Iran is dragging down President Trump's approval rating, with just 37% of American adults approving of his performance. The poll reflects growing public concern about the prolonged conflict.

headlines.headline: Most Voters Blame Trump for Rising Gas Prices, Quinnipiac Poll Shows
headlines.summary: Most registered voters blame President Trump for rising gas prices, according to a new Quinnipiac University national poll. The survey highlights the political cost of the Iran war's impact on energy prices ahead of the midterm elections.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Rebound 7% as Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Again
headlines.summary: Oil prices rebounded more than 7% on Monday after tumbling more than 9% on Friday as Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz again, reversing a brief reopening. Brent crude futures jumped $6.56 to $96.94 a barrel as the vital waterway remains blocked.

headlines.headline: EV Sales Soar in Europe as Iran War Drives Petrol Prices to Record Highs
headlines.summary: Sales of fully electric cars in Europe's main auto markets jumped by almost a third in the first quarter of 2026, as drivers sought alternatives to combustion engines after the Iran war caused the highest spike in petrol prices in years.

headlines.headline: Bangladesh Raises Fuel Prices 10-15% as Iran War Drives Up Global Costs
headlines.summary: Bangladesh raised retail fuel prices by 10% to 15%, citing a sharp surge in global crude oil prices and tightening supplies caused by the ongoing Middle East conflict. The energy ministry announced the increases late Saturday as the Iran war continues to ripple through global energy markets.

headlines.headline: Two Foreigners Arrested in Iran for Importing Starlink Technology
headlines.summary: Iranian authorities arrested four individuals, including two foreign nationals, in the country's northwest on charges of being part of a 'U.S.-Israel-linked espionage network' for importing Starlink satellite internet technology. The arrests highlight Iran's continued crackdown on unauthorized communications technology.

headlines.headline: US Energy Chief Warns Gas Prices Could Stay Above $3 Until Next Year
headlines.summary: The US Energy Secretary warned that gasoline prices could remain above $3 per gallon through next year, with the average price at $4.05 per gallon compared to $3.16 a year ago. The Iran war's disruption of global oil supplies continues to pressure American consumers.

headlines.headline: Investors Return to 'TINA' Trades as US-Iran Ceasefire Revives Stock Market Hopes
headlines.summary: The US-Iran ceasefire has revived 'TINA' (There Is No Alternative) stock market trades, driven by peace hopes, soaring US earnings growth, and the relative insulation of the American economy from energy shocks. Investors are piling into US stocks as recession fears ease.

headlines.headline: DHS Shutdown Raises Security Concerns for FIFA World Cup 50 Days Away
headlines.summary: With the FIFA World Cup just over 50 days away, questions are mounting about whether the Department of Homeland Security shutdown will affect security preparations for the tournament. Former DHS officials express concerns about readiness amid political uncertainty.

headlines.headline: India Brings 500 MW Sodium-Cooled Nuclear Reactor Online Using Thorium
headlines.summary: India has brought a 500 MW sodium-cooled reactor online at Kalpakkam, leveraging its abundant domestic thorium resources for energy security. The country plans to build 18 standard design reactors as part of a state-led nuclear program with full control over the value chain.

headlines.headline: Japan's Energy Diplomacy Runs Into China's Head Start in Central Asia, Latin America
headlines.summary: As Japan steps up energy diplomacy to secure alternatives to Middle Eastern oil and gas, Tokyo is finding that China has already established a significant lead in Central Asia and Latin America. The competition highlights shifting energy geopolitics as nations seek to diversify away from the volatile Middle East.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Voices Solidarity With Ukraine as Civilian Attacks Escalate
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV expressed solidarity with Ukraine amid escalating attacks against civilians, saying he was 'deeply saddened' by the recent escalation. The Pope renewed his appeal for 'weapons to fall silent' after Russia launched a mass missile attack that killed at least 17 civilians.

headlines.headline: Iranian Official Says Over 20 Passenger Planes Damaged in US-Israeli Attacks
headlines.summary: An Iranian aviation official said more than 20 passenger planes were damaged in US-Israeli attacks, though reports of wider damage were inaccurate as most airport capacity remains intact. The statement comes amid ongoing conflict that has disrupted regional aviation.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria's Radev Claims 'Uncontested Victory' as Progressive Coalition Sweeps Elections
headlines.summary: Former President Rumen Radev's Progressive Bulgaria coalition claimed victory with 37-38% of the vote, sweeping aside the center-right GERB party that dominated politics for the last decade. The victory comes after mass protests brought down the government in December.

headlines.headline: Canada's Carney Says Economic Ties With US Have Become a Weakness
headlines.summary: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said the country's strong economic ties to the United States were once a strength but are now a weakness that must be corrected, citing Trump's tariffs that have affected auto and steel workers.

headlines.headline: Israeli Ministers Celebrate Re-establishment of West Bank Settlement Two Decades After Eviction
headlines.summary: Israeli Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich and other officials attended a ceremony reopening the Sa-Nur settlement in the occupied West Bank, 21 years after it was evacuated. The move is part of Israel's reversal of its 2005 disengagement policy.

headlines.headline: US and Iran Accuse Each Other of Ceasefire Violations as Hormuz Standoff Continues
headlines.summary: President Trump accused Iran of firing on French and British ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz, while Iran's foreign ministry called the US blockade a 'war crime.' Indian-flagged vessels were among those attacked, prompting India to summon Iran's ambassador.

headlines.headline: Allies Fear Rushed US-Iran Framework Deal Could Backfire and Entrench Problems
headlines.summary: Diplomats worry that a headline-grabbing framework agreement between Washington and Tehran could entrench deeper problems, leaving technical issues unresolved. The concerns come as US negotiators prepare for talks in Islamabad.

headlines.headline: Iran Denies Agreeing to New Talks as Trump Claims Negotiations Continue
headlines.summary: Iran denied it had agreed to new nuclear talks, contradicting Trump's claim that negotiations would continue. The denial came amid escalating tensions over the Strait of Hormuz and a US naval blockade.

headlines.headline: Merz and Lula Stress European-Brazilian Cooperation Amid Global Uncertainty
headlines.summary: German Chancellor Merz and Brazilian President Lula emphasized close cooperation between Europe and Brazil, discussing trade, climate policy, and multilateral institutions amid shifting global alliances.

headlines.headline: Eight Children Killed in Louisiana Mass Shooting, Police Say
headlines.summary: Eight children between the ages of 1 and 14 were killed in a mass shooting in Shreveport, Louisiana. Police are investigating the incident as one of the deadliest attacks on children in recent U.S. history.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Pushes for Europe to Build Defense System Against Ballistic Weapons
headlines.summary: Ukraine is urging European nations to develop a joint missile defense system to counter ballistic threats, as Russia intensifies attacks on Ukrainian cities and infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Israeli Military Publishes Map of South Lebanon Territory Under Its Control
headlines.summary: The Israeli military published for the first time a map of its new deployment line inside Lebanon, bringing dozens of mostly abandoned Lebanese villages under its control just days after a ceasefire with Hezbollah took effect. The move signals Israel's intention to maintain a security buffer zone despite the truce.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Urges Angola to Heal Divisions at Mass Drawing 130,000
headlines.summary: Pope Leo urged Angolans to overcome divisions after decades of war, first at a Mass in a field outside Luanda and then in a prayer at a site that was once a hub for transatlantic slavery. The events jointly drew roughly 130,000 people as the Pope continued his Africa tour.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria's Pro-Russian Former President Takes Strong Lead in Election
headlines.summary: The party of Bulgaria's pro-Russian former President Rumen Radev is set to win Sunday's parliamentary vote with 37.5% of the votes according to exit polls. Radev, who opposes sanctions against Russia and military aid for Ukraine, campaigned to stamp out corruption and end a spiral of weak governments.

headlines.headline: Trump Says US Navy Seizes Iranian Cargo Ship Defying Hormuz Blockade
headlines.summary: President Trump announced the U.S. Navy forcibly seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship, the Touska, that tried to bypass the naval blockade near the Strait of Hormuz. A Navy destroyer "blew a hole in the engineroom" after the vessel refused to stop.

headlines.headline: North Korea Fires Short-Range Ballistic Missiles as UN Warns of Nuclear Advances
headlines.summary: North Korea launched multiple short-range ballistic missiles from its Sinpo area, with projectiles flying about 140 kilometers toward eastern waters. The test came days after the UN nuclear watchdog warned Pyongyang is making 'very serious' advances in its nuclear weapons program, marking the seventh missile test this year.

headlines.headline: Asian Development Bank Chief Warns of Yen Pressure From Japan's Slow Rate Hikes
headlines.summary: ADB President Masato Kanda warned that the Japanese yen could face further pressure if markets perceive the Bank of Japan as moving too slowly on addressing inflationary risks. The comments highlight ongoing currency tensions as global central banks navigate post-war economic uncertainty.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Calls for 'Weapons to Fall Silent' as Ukraine War Intensifies
headlines.summary: Pope Leo decried the intensification of the war in Ukraine, calling 'for the weapons to fall silent and for the path of dialogue to be followed.' The Pope's appeal comes amid escalating Russian drone attacks and Ukrainian strikes on Russian military infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Russian Strikes Kill Two in Ukraine as Kyiv Hits Russian Drone Factory
headlines.summary: Russian drone attacks killed a 16-year-old boy in Chernihiv and a man in Kherson, while Ukraine's navy struck the Atlant Aero drone factory in Taganrog using Neptune cruise missiles. The facility develops strike and reconnaissance drones and components for UAVs carrying guided bombs.

headlines.headline: Belarus's Lukashenko Says Ready to Meet Trump Once 'Big Deal' Is Prepared
headlines.summary: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko said he would be ready to meet US President Donald Trump once a 'big deal' between the two countries has been prepared. The statement signals potential for Belarus-West diplomacy as the country remains a key Russian ally in the Ukraine conflict.

headlines.headline: IMF and World Bank Meetings Show Limits of Crisis Response Without US Leadership
headlines.summary: Spring meetings of the IMF and World Bank revealed the limits of global institutions in mitigating shocks from the Iran war and other crises, showing that counting on US leadership to resolve global problems is no longer the guarantee it once was. The meetings highlighted a shift in the global financial order.

headlines.headline: Spain and Mexico End Diplomatic Spat Over Colonial Past With Handshake
headlines.summary: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum laid to rest a years-long diplomatic dispute with Spain over the Spanish colonial past during her visit to Barcelona. The reconciliation follows King Felipe VI's March acknowledgment of abuses during the conquest of the Americas, ending tensions that began in 2019.

headlines.headline: EU Leaders Back Major New Defense Spending Plans at Ukraine Summit
headlines.summary: European Union leaders committed to a massive step in defense cooperation following decades of hesitation, spurred by the Ukraine conflict and uncertainty about US commitment to NATO. The summit marks a significant shift toward European defense autonomy.

headlines.headline: US Backs South Africa Rare Earths Project to Counter China's Mineral Dominance
headlines.summary: The Trump administration is supporting a $50 million project to extract rare earth elements from mining waste in South Africa, part of accelerated US efforts to reduce reliance on China for minerals crucial to electronics, defense systems, and electric vehicles. The project continues despite a major diplomatic rift between Washington and Pretoria.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Downplays Feud With Trump, Says 'Not in My Interest' to Debate
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV sought to downplay his feud with US President Donald Trump, saying reporting about his comments during his Africa tour 'has not been accurate in all its aspects.' The Pope said it is 'not in my interest at all' to debate Trump but will continue preaching peace.

headlines.headline: UK Seeks EU Reset as Iran War Strains Ties With Trump's America
headlines.summary: Britain's government is set to announce legislation next month to move the country closer to the European Union, as the Iran war and Trump's unpredictability sour the UK's 'special relationship' with the United States. Prime Minister Keir Starmer's EU 'reset' bill would give ministers powers to align UK standards with EU single market rules.

headlines.headline: Mediation Fails in Dispute Over Franco-German Fighter Jet Project
headlines.summary: Mediators have failed to resolve a dispute between companies involved in the Franco-German FCAS fighter jet project, Handelsblatt reported. The setback threatens the Future Combat Air System program, a flagship European defense collaboration aimed at developing next-generation fighter aircraft.

headlines.headline: Turkey Warns US Withdrawal From European Security Would Be 'Destructive'
headlines.summary: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan said discussions are underway on managing a possible US withdrawal from the European security architecture, warning such a move would be 'destructive' for regional stability. The comments reflect growing concern among NATO allies about America's commitment to the alliance.

headlines.headline: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Again as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
headlines.summary: Iran has once again closed the Strait of Hormuz, blaming US 'breaches of trust' for the reversal just one day after announcing the waterway would reopen. IRGC gunboats fired on tankers attempting to pass through, while Washington and Tehran remain 'far from a final agreement' on nuclear talks.

headlines.headline: South Africa Central Bank Chief Says Iran War Clouds Rate Cut Prospects
headlines.summary: South Africa's central bank governor told Reuters that it was difficult to see a near-term path for easing interest rates due to volatility from the war in the Middle East and its impact on inflation. The comments highlight how the Iran conflict is rippling through global monetary policy.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria Bombarded by Pro-Russian Propaganda Ahead of Elections
headlines.summary: Bulgaria is being flooded with pro-Russian propaganda ahead of Sunday's parliamentary election, with around 600 pro-Kremlin websites spreading disinformation. The campaign comes as polls show pro-Russian former President Rumen Radev as the frontrunner.

headlines.headline: Humanoid Robot Beats Human Record in Beijing Half-Marathon, Showcasing China's Tech Advances
headlines.summary: A Chinese-made humanoid robot completed a half-marathon in Beijing faster than the human world record, finishing in 50 minutes and 26 seconds. The race featured dozens of robots demonstrating rapidly improving athleticism and autonomous navigation skills, highlighting China's advances in humanoid robotics.

headlines.headline: Hungary's Election Winner Magyar Increases Parliamentary Majority After Final Vote Count
headlines.summary: Hungarian election winner Peter Magyar's Tisza party increased its parliamentary supermajority to 141 seats out of 199 after final vote counting. The result solidifies the defeat of Viktor Orbán's Fidesz party and marks a significant shift in Hungarian politics after 14 years of illiberal rule.

headlines.headline: Brazil, Mexico and Spain Pledge Coordinated Aid to Cuba Amid US Embargo Pressure
headlines.summary: The governments of Brazil, Spain and Mexico vowed to step up coordinated aid to Cuba to alleviate what they described as a humanitarian crisis caused by the US blockade of the Caribbean island. The pledge came during a progressive summit in Barcelona that drew condemnation from President Trump.

headlines.headline: British Foreign Secretary Calls for Full Resumption of Hormuz Shipping
headlines.summary: British Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper said the Strait of Hormuz had yet to return to normal operations despite a ceasefire in the Iran war, urging Tehran to allow global shipping to fully resume. The call comes as vessels continue to face uncertainty in the vital waterway.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian Missiles Strike Russian Drone Plant in Taganrog
headlines.summary: A drone manufacturing plant in the southern Russian city of Taganrog caught fire after being struck by Ukrainian missiles overnight. The strike represents Ukraine's continued targeting of Russian military infrastructure as the conflict enters its third year.

headlines.headline: Russia Strikes Zaporizhzhia Region 756 Times in 24 Hours, One Injured
headlines.summary: Russian forces carried out 756 strikes on 44 settlements in Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia region over 24 hours, injuring at least one person. The bombardment continues the pattern of intense Russian attacks on southeastern Ukraine despite ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Chernobyl's Wildlife Thrives Four Decades After Nuclear Disaster
headlines.summary: Four decades after the nuclear disaster at Ukraine's Chernobyl power plant, wildlife is thriving again in the exclusion zone. Przewalski's horses and other species have returned to the radioactive landscape, demonstrating nature's resilience in areas now too dangerous for humans.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria Votes as Pro-Russian Former President Leads Polls in Eighth Election in Five Years
headlines.summary: Bulgarians head to the polls for the eighth time in five years, with pro-Russian former President Rumen Radev leading the race. The vote comes after mass protests brought down the conservative-led government in December, with voters frustrated by endemic corruption and weak coalition governments.

headlines.headline: China Positions Itself as Pillar of Stability as US Credibility Declines
headlines.summary: Beijing is capitalizing on US missteps in the Iran war to improve its global image, positioning China as a pillar of stability in contrast to American volatility. The contrast between China's orderly Five-Year Plan approach and US disruption is reshaping how the world views the two superpowers.

headlines.headline: Iran War Analysis: What Happens If America Loses?
headlines.summary: The 2026 Iran War marks a turning point in modern geopolitics, demonstrating how the West's attempts to maintain dominance through military intervention destabilize the region and hasten the rise of a multipolar world order. The conflict has trapped the US in an 'escalation trap' with significant consequences for global energy and financial systems.

headlines.headline: Malaysia Condemns Attack on UN Peacekeepers in Lebanon, Calls for Security Council Action
headlines.summary: Malaysia condemned the deadly attack on UNIFIL peacekeepers in Lebanon that killed one French soldier and wounded three others, calling on the UN Security Council to ensure the safety of peacekeepers. Malaysia's Malbatt troops deployed under UNIFIL are reported safe.

headlines.headline: Pakistan's Army Chief Emerges as Key Power Broker in US-Iran Negotiations
headlines.summary: While Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif traveled to regional capitals, Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir went to Iran to continue mediation efforts after Islamabad hosted Washington and Tehran for high-level talks. The dynamic reveals the military's central role in Pakistan's foreign policy.

headlines.headline: North Korea Fires Multiple Ballistic Missiles in Seventh Launch This Year
headlines.summary: North Korea fired multiple ballistic missiles toward the sea off its east coast, South Korea and Japan said, marking Pyongyang's seventh missile test of 2026. The launches come as tensions remain high on the Korean Peninsula amid concerns about North Korea's accelerating weapons development.

headlines.headline: Trump and Iran Cite Progress in Talks but Hormuz Uncertainty Persists
headlines.summary: Both Trump and Iran signaled progress in nuclear negotiations as a 10-day ceasefire nears expiration, but uncertainty hangs over the Strait of Hormuz after Tehran reversed its decision to reopen the waterway. The war, now in its eighth week, has killed thousands and sent oil prices surging.

headlines.headline: Iran's Navy Warns Ships to Stay Out of Hormuz, Two Vessels Report Gunfire
headlines.summary: Merchant vessels attempting to cross the Strait of Hormuz received radio messages from Iran's navy telling them they were not allowed to pass, while two ships reported being hit by gunfire. The incidents mark a dangerous escalation as Tehran reimposed its blockade.

headlines.headline: Iran's Hard-Liners Flex Muscle with Hormuz U-Turn, Complicating US Diplomacy
headlines.summary: Divisions between Iranian moderates and the Revolutionary Guard are complicating US efforts for a diplomatic win, analysts say. The hard-liners' reversal on reopening the Strait of Hormuz demonstrates their political power and raises questions about who controls Iran's negotiating position.

headlines.headline: Two Indian Ships Come Under Fire as Iran Reasserts Hormuz Control
headlines.summary: Iran fired on two Indian-flagged vessels in the Strait of Hormuz after reimposing strict control on the waterway, just one day after announcing its reopening. The attacks prompted India to summon Iran's ambassador in protest as New Delhi navigates the crisis.

headlines.headline: Bangladesh Raises Fuel Prices as Iran War Drives Up Costs
headlines.summary: Bangladesh raised fuel prices as the Iran war drives up crude costs, supply chain disruptions and higher freight and insurance rates. Officials said the increase was unavoidable as the conflict continues to ripple through global energy markets.

headlines.headline: Japan and Australia Sign Contracts for $7 Billion Warship Deal
headlines.summary: Australia and Japan signed contracts launching a landmark deal to supply Australia with Japanese-designed Mogami-class stealth frigates. The A$10 billion agreement marks Tokyo's most significant defense export since ending its military export ban in 2014 and signals Japan's emerging role as a global arms exporter.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Denounced 'Despots and Tyrants' in Forceful Angola Address
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV sharply decried the exploitation of natural resources in Africa during his Angola visit, blasting 'despots and tyrants' who guarantee wealth but deliver suffering. He also clarified that his remarks about tyrants were not aimed at Trump specifically.

headlines.headline: US Approves $11.9 Billion AEGIS Naval Combat Systems Sale to Germany
headlines.summary: The State Department approved a potential $11.9 billion sale of AEGIS-based integrated combat systems, SPY-6 radars, and associated equipment for eight German F127 frigates. The deal strengthens NATO interoperability as European allies accelerate defense investments amid uncertainty about US commitment to the alliance.

headlines.headline: Iran War Energy Shock Drives Nuclear Power Plans Across Asia and Africa
headlines.summary: The Iran war's global energy shock is pushing nations in Asia and Africa to accelerate nuclear power development, with non-nuclear countries now planning atomic energy programs. The IEA warned Europe may have only 'six weeks of jet fuel left' as the conflict disrupts traditional energy supplies.

headlines.headline: EU Launches Collective Defense Simulations Amid NATO Uncertainty
headlines.summary: The European Union is conducting tabletop simulations to test Article 42.7 mutual defense clause, starting with Brussels ambassadors and defense ministers in Cyprus. The exercises come as Europe prepares for greater defense autonomy should US support for NATO diminish.

headlines.headline: Zelenskyy Warns Putin Will Try to Involve Belarus in Ukraine War
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy warned that Russia is once again trying to drag Belarus into the war, cautioning Alexander Lukashenko's regime against making mistakes. The warning comes as Belarus remains a key Russian ally in the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Iran Says Uranium 'Not Going Anywhere' After Trump Claims Deal Struck
headlines.summary: Iran insisted its uranium was 'not going anywhere' despite President Trump's claims that a deal had been reached for the US to remove nuclear material. The contradiction highlights ongoing gaps in US-Iran negotiations over the nuclear program.

headlines.headline: Japan and Australia Sign $7 Billion Warship Deal in Largest Postwar Defense Export
headlines.summary: Australia and Japan signed contracts launching a landmark deal to supply Australia with Japanese-designed Mogami-class stealth frigates. The agreement marks Tokyo's most significant defense export since World War II and signals a shift in Japan's role as a global military exporter amid China concerns.

headlines.headline: US Installs Trump Loyalist to Lead Grand Jury Probe of President's Political Enemies
headlines.summary: A former lawyer for President Trump's campaign, Joseph diGenova, is reportedly planning to split time between Miami and Fort Pierce, where a grand jury overseen by a Trump-favored judge sits. The move raises concerns about the use of federal law enforcement to target political opponents.

headlines.headline: Lebanese President Says Ceasefire Should Move to Permanent Agreements
headlines.summary: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun said the current ceasefire should be transformed into 'permanent agreements,' signaling potential interest in a more durable settlement. The statement comes as the 10-day Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire holds despite tensions.

headlines.headline: New Zealand and US Wargame Scenario of Russian Nuclear Attack on Satellites
headlines.summary: The New Zealand Defence Force participated in a classified wargame with the US based on a Russian nuclear blast aimed at taking out satellites. The exercise was run by the American space warfighting agency alongside 60 companies, highlighting growing concerns about space warfare.

headlines.headline: Trump Administration Asks Ford and GM to Boost Military Production Amid Wars
headlines.summary: The Pentagon is in talks with General Motors, Ford and other manufacturers to increase weapons and munitions production as wars in Iran and Ukraine strain US military supplies. The discussions reflect concerns about industrial capacity for sustained conflicts.

headlines.headline: Colombia's Petro Warns of Latin American 'Rebellion' If US Doesn't Rethink Policy
headlines.summary: Colombian President Gustavo Petro warned that US policy could spark a Latin American rebellion as he criticized OFAC sanctions and called for rethinking ties. The comments come amid regional tensions over Cuba and Venezuela policies.

headlines.headline: World Bank Reclassifies Pakistan to MENA Region After Mediation Role
headlines.summary: The World Bank has quietly reclassified Pakistan out of the South Asia regional grouping to the MENA region beginning fiscal year 2026. The move follows Islamabad's emergence as a key mediator in the Iran-US conflict and reflects its growing role in Middle East diplomacy.

headlines.headline: North Korea Fires Multiple Ballistic Missiles, Putting Japan and South Korea on Alert
headlines.summary: North Korea launched multiple ballistic missiles from its eastern Sinpo area toward the sea, South Korea's military said. The launches mark Pyongyang's seventh ballistic missile test this year and come amid heightened regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Hezbollah Lays Out Difficult Conditions for Extending Ceasefire with Israel
headlines.summary: Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem said the group is 'fully open' to cooperating with the Lebanese government on a ceasefire, but laid out five difficult conditions including permanent cessation of Israeli attacks and complete troops withdrawal. The group says it will keep fighters ready for any violations.

headlines.headline: US and Cuban Officials Hold Rare Talks in Havana Amid Tensions
headlines.summary: An American delegation met with Cuban government officials in Havana in a renewed diplomatic push, even as President Trump has threatened to intervene in the country. The talks included a proposal to provide Starlink satellite internet to the island in return for economic and political reforms.

headlines.headline: Iran War Has Shifted Perceptions of US Power, Analysts Say
headlines.summary: The Iran war has revealed a superpower being led by 'impulsiveness and strategic incompetence,' says Harvard's Stephen Walt. The conflict demonstrated how a weaker adversary can use asymmetric warfare and economic choke points to resist American military might, raising concerns among US allies.

headlines.headline: Trump's Feud with Spain's Sánchez Gives Embattled Leader a Political Lifeline
headlines.summary: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez has emerged as a progressive hero abroad for standing up to President Trump, while at home Trump's attacks are seen as helping Sánchez distract from domestic scandals and survive politically ahead of next year's election.

headlines.headline: Europe Expects Ukraine War to Last Until 2030, Belgian Military Chief Warns
headlines.summary: Belgian General Staff Chief Frederik Vansina said Europe has a limited window to build deterrence as the war in Ukraine could last until 2030. The warning comes as European nations accelerate defense planning for scenarios without full US support.

headlines.headline: Spain, Brazil and Mexico Pledge to Boost Aid to Cuba Amid US Threats
headlines.summary: Leftist leaders meeting in Barcelona vowed to increase aid to Cuba amid a US oil embargo and President Trump's threats to invade the island. The pledge came during a progressive summit that drew condemnation from Trump on social media.

headlines.headline: NATO Chief Rutte Dismisses Speculation About US Leaving Alliance
headlines.summary: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said he understands Donald Trump's frustration with NATO while dismissing speculation about a US withdrawal. He called on Europe to boost its defense industry as the alliance faces questions about American commitment.

headlines.headline: Audio Captures Desperate Plea from Indian Tanker Captain Under Iranian Fire in Hormuz
headlines.summary: Audio recordings reveal the moment an Indian oil tanker captain begged 'let me turn back' as Iranian forces opened fire in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran fired on two Indian-flagged vessels, prompting New Delhi to summon Tehran's ambassador in protest.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu 'Shocked' Trump Prohibited Israel Strikes on Lebanon, Seeks Clarification
headlines.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu was reportedly shocked by Trump's public remarks prohibiting strikes on Lebanon, believing the statement exceeded the terms of the US-backed ceasefire. Israeli officials are seeking clarification from Washington.

headlines.headline: Five Loaded Qatari LNG Vessels Approach Strait of Hormuz as Closure Looms
headlines.summary: Five vessels loaded with liquefied natural gas from Qatar's Ras Laffan terminal are approaching the Strait of Hormuz, ship-tracking data showed. The vessels face uncertainty as Iran reimposed restrictions on the vital waterway.

headlines.headline: WFP Warns Over Half of Haiti's Population Faces Worsening Hunger Crisis
headlines.summary: The World Food Programme warned that more than 5.8 million Haitians — approximately 52% of the country's population — are facing acute hunger as the crisis-wracked Caribbean nation struggles with violence and political instability.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo XIV Says 'Not in My Interest' to Debate Trump, Will Keep Preaching Peace
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV said it is 'not in my interest at all' to debate President Trump about the war in Iran but that he will continue preaching the Gospel message of peace. The Pope spoke to reporters during his Africa tour in Angola.

headlines.headline: US-Iran Deal Still Far from Reach as Major Gaps Remain on Enrichment
headlines.summary: Analysis suggests a US-Iran deal remains distant despite ongoing negotiations. Major gaps persist on uranium enrichment levels, while Iran uses Hormuz leverage to push for security guarantees and sanctions relief.

headlines.headline: IMF Warns Global Economy on Brink of Recession Amid Wars and Energy Crisis
headlines.summary: The International Monetary Fund issued a stark warning that the global economy is 'threatened with being thrown off course' due to ongoing conflicts and soaring energy prices. The report highlights growing stagflation risks as the Iran war disrupts oil supplies and markets worldwide.

headlines.headline: US Lawmaker Launches Investigation into Jared Kushner's Business Ties and Diplomatic Role
headlines.summary: Democratic Representative Jamie Raskin opened a 'sweeping' investigation into President Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner over potential conflicts of interest. Kushner has been mediating Ukraine-Russia and Iran negotiations while running a private equity firm with potential financial stakes in the outcomes.

headlines.headline: CENTCOM Reveals 13 US Troops Killed, Nearly 400 Wounded in Iran War
headlines.summary: The US Central Command disclosed that 13 American troops have been killed and nearly 400 wounded in the Iran conflict. CENTCOM spokesman Tim Hawkins said 354 of the wounded have returned to duty, providing the first comprehensive casualty count since the war began.

headlines.headline: HSBC and Standard Chartered Linked to Multimillion-Pound Iran Money Laundering Scheme
headlines.summary: Two of Britain's largest banks have become embroiled in allegations of a multimillion-pound scheme to launder money for Iran. The allegations come as Western nations maintain strict sanctions on Tehran amid the ongoing conflict over the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: UK Startup to Test Acoustic Sensor System Against Russian Drones in Ukraine
headlines.summary: British defense startup Arcani plans to test its HARK acoustic sensor system in Ukraine, marking another instance of Western defense technology being deployed in the conflict. The passive acoustic reconnaissance kit is designed to detect hostile drones and incoming threats.

headlines.headline: US Forces Turn Back 23 Iranian Vessels as Naval Blockade Tightens
headlines.summary: The US military reported that 23 ships have complied with orders to turn back toward Iran since the American blockade of Iranian ports began. The enforcement action is part of the 'Economic Fury' campaign to pressure Tehran over the Strait of Hormuz closure.

headlines.headline: Trump Signs Executive Order Speeding Psychedelic Drug Reviews for PTSD Treatment
headlines.summary: President Trump directed his administration to expedite reviews of psychedelic drugs, including ibogaine, for treating mental health conditions. The order follows advocacy from veterans groups and conservative lawmakers seeking alternatives for PTSD treatment.

headlines.headline: Trump Organization Plans 70-Story Skyscraper in Tbilisi, Georgia
headlines.summary: The Trump Organization and partners plan to build a 70-story 'Trump Tower' in Georgia's capital, the Wall Street Journal reported. The project comes as President Trump faces scrutiny over potential conflicts between his business interests and foreign policy decisions.

headlines.headline: Venezuela's Machado Says 'No Regrets' About Giving Nobel Prize to Trump
headlines.summary: Exiled opposition leader María Corina Machado defended her decision to symbolically gift her Nobel Peace Prize to Trump, calling him a leader who 'risked the lives of his country's citizens' for Venezuela's freedom. She remains in contact with the Trump administration about returning to Venezuela.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo XIV Challenges Angola's Leaders to End Africa's Exploitation
headlines.summary: During his Africa tour, Pope Leo IV called on Angola's leaders to 'break the cycles of interests' that have plundered and exploited Africa, blasting 'despots and tyrants' who make false promises. He dismissed media narratives of a rift with Trump over the Iran war.

headlines.headline: India's Defence Minister Reviews West Asia Situation Amid Hormuz Crisis
headlines.summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh chaired a meeting in New Delhi to assess India's preparedness as the Strait of Hormuz crisis escalates. The review comes after Iran fired on two Indian tankers and India approved a $1.4 billion maritime insurance pool.

headlines.headline: Trump Convenes Situation Room Briefing as Iran Chokes Off World Oil Supply
headlines.summary: President Trump called an emergency meeting with his inner circle, including JD Vance, Marco Rubio, and Pete Hegseth, to discuss the Strait of Hormuz crisis. The briefing came after Iran reimposed restrictions on the vital waterway.

headlines.headline: Trump, IRS in Talks to Settle President's $10 Billion Lawsuit Over Leaked Tax Returns
headlines.summary: Lawyers for Donald Trump and the IRS are negotiating a settlement of the president's $10 billion lawsuit against the tax agency for leaking his tax returns to media outlets in 2019 and 2020. The case has drawn attention to privacy protections for taxpayer information.

headlines.headline: Japan Embraces Nuclear Power Post-Fukushima as AI Drives Energy Demand
headlines.summary: Japan is rapidly moving to restart nuclear reactors as artificial intelligence increases electricity demand and foreign wars reshape energy security thinking. The country is reconsidering its post-Fukushima nuclear phase-out as it faces growing power needs.

headlines.headline: Iran's Resistance Offers Lessons for Taiwan, Security Analysts Say
headlines.summary: Iran's ability to prevent a decisive US victory despite inferior weapons has implications for Taiwan's defense strategy. Analysts note that decentralized command, dispersed weapons, and large drone/missile stockpiles helped Iran close the Strait of Hormuz and withstand strikes.

headlines.headline: Kyiv Gunman Was Russian-Born, Zelenskyy Calls Shooting 'Act of Terrorism'
headlines.summary: Ukraine's security service labeled the Kyiv supermarket attack as terrorism after the 58-year-old gunman was identified as Russian-born. The shooter had a valid weapons permit, prior criminal record, and had lived in the Donetsk region before killing six and wounding 14.

headlines.headline: Trump Says China's Xi Is 'Very Happy' About Hormuz Status Ahead of China Visit
headlines.summary: President Trump said Chinese President Xi Jinping was 'very happy' about developments with the Strait of Hormuz and reiterated he looks forward to their meeting in China next month. The trip was delayed from March due to the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Russia Strikes Ukrainian Port and Power Grid, Ukraine Hits Russian Oil Facilities
headlines.summary: Russian attacks overnight damaged port infrastructure in Ukraine's Odesa region and caused blackouts for 380,000 consumers in the north. Ukraine struck back with drone attacks on oil refineries in Samara, a Crimea oil depot, and a Baltic Sea petroleum port.

headlines.headline: New Zealand Defends Military Patrol Flight Near China After Beijing's Protest
headlines.summary: New Zealand defended the actions of a military patrol aircraft flight near China after Beijing said it had undermined its security interests. The incident highlights growing tensions in the Pacific as Western nations increase surveillance operations.

headlines.headline: US Renews Russian Oil Sanctions Waiver Amid Iran War Price Shocks
headlines.summary: The Trump administration renewed a waiver allowing countries to buy sanctioned Russian oil at sea for about a month, responding to pressure from nations dealing with oil price volatility from the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Six Killed in Kyiv Shooting and Hostage Incident, Police Neutralize Gunman
headlines.summary: A gunman opened fire on passersby in a Kyiv district before barricading himself in a supermarket with hostages. Ukrainian police killed the suspect, but six people died in the incident.

headlines.headline: India Approves $1.4 Billion Maritime Insurance Pool Amid War Disruptions
headlines.summary: India approved a 129.8-billion-rupee guarantee for a maritime insurance pool as wars and sanctions prompt insurers to withdraw cover, threatening trade flows. The move comes after Indian tankers were fired upon in the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: US Mine-Clearing Capabilities Questioned as Strait of Hormuz Threat Looms
headlines.summary: Analysts are raising doubts about America's ability to clear mines from the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting a gap in US naval capabilities and reinforcing the need for NATO allies in mine countermeasure operations.

headlines.headline: Israel Enforces 'Yellow Line' Buffer Zone in Lebanon, Strikes Approaching Individuals
headlines.summary: The Israeli army publicly referred to a 'Yellow Line' for the first time, described as a self-declared buffer zone in Lebanon. Israel said it targeted individuals approaching the zone, marking an escalation in ceasefire enforcement.

headlines.headline: French Peacekeeper Killed in Lebanon Attack During Ceasefire
headlines.summary: A French UNIFIL peacekeeper was killed and three others wounded in a small arms attack near Ghandouriyeh in southern Lebanon. French President Macron blamed Hezbollah, which denied involvement. The attack came just after a 10-day Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire took effect.

headlines.headline: Trump Predicts 'Special' Meeting with Xi Jinping Ahead of China Visit
headlines.summary: President Trump said he looks forward to meeting President Xi Jinping in China next month, predicting the summit would be 'historic' and achieve much. The trip was delayed from March due to the Iran war. Analysts say the meeting could stabilize China-US relations through 2029.

headlines.headline: China Could Gain 'Massive Strategic Advantage' from Iran War, Analyst Says
headlines.summary: A Chinese analyst said Beijing should draw military lessons from the Iran conflict, noting that US engagement in warfare narrows the equipment gap between Chinese and American forces. With the ceasefire likely to extend, China may assume a more active role in peace talks.

headlines.headline: Venezuela's Machado Draws Thousands in Madrid, Declines Meeting with Spain's PM
headlines.summary: Exiled Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado rallied thousands in Madrid while PM Sánchez hosted a progressive summit in Barcelona. Machado praised Trump for removing Maduro and said she declined meeting Sánchez because of the leftist gathering.
